title: Ocrolus vs. MoneyThumb vs. StatementScrub: A Practical Comparison
meta: Compare leading bank statement analysis tools on speed, pricing, and fit for MCA brokers and lenders. See where each fits and which suits high-volume underwriting.
content:
Choosing a Bank Statement Analysis Tool

Ocrolus and MoneyThumb are two of the best-known names in document and bank statement analysis, each built for a different buyer. Here's a practical, honest look at how they differ and where a focused tool fits for MCA brokers and small-business lenders.

Ocrolus

Ocrolus is an enterprise-grade document automation platform used by larger fintech lenders, with broad document coverage and API-first integration. It is powerful and configurable, but typically carries enterprise pricing and onboarding — well-suited to high-volume institutions with engineering resources, less so to a small broker shop wanting a quick read.

MoneyThumb

MoneyThumb is a long-established tool popular for converting and analyzing statements, with desktop and API options. It is strong on PDF conversion and has a loyal user base in mortgage and small-business lending. Its analysis is more rule-based than conversational AI, and pricing is generally per-document or subscription.

Where a Focused Tool Fits

For MCA brokers and small lenders, the priorities are speed, a clear risk read (NSF, MCA detection, negative days), and lender matching — without enterprise overhead. StatementScrub is built around that exact workflow: upload a PDF, get income, NSF, MCA, fraud signals, a risk score, and a recommendation in under 30 seconds, plus matching against 435+ lenders.

How to Decide

  • Enterprise fintech with dev resources: Ocrolus.
  • PDF conversion + established mortgage workflows: MoneyThumb.
  • Fast MCA/small-business underwriting + lender matching: a focused analyzer like StatementScrub.

Bottom Line

The right tool depends on volume, integration needs, and whether you need lender matching built in. Try a few real statements through each and compare the speed and clarity of the output.
